text.title: Jira via Atlassian Rovo MCP, Permission-Inheriting Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the tracker-jira-rovo-mcp-agents methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every AI-to-Jira/Confluence/JSM access MUST go through the Rovo Remote MCP server. Direct Jira REST API calls from AI clients are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Direct API calls bypass permission inheritance + audit trail; centralizing through Rovo MCP is what makes the access governable.

rule.statement: The Rovo MCP server MUST run actions as the inviting Jira user, inheriting that user's project permissions. Service-account impersonation that exceeds the user's scope is forbidden.
rule.rationale: If the agent can exceed user permissions, the audit trail lies about who did what.

rule.statement: Every AI action MUST appear in the native Jira audit log with the inviting user's identity + the AI client name. Custom audit channels are not a substitute.
rule.rationale: Compliance reviews query the native log; bespoke channels are invisible to them.

rule.statement: MCP scope MUST be limited to projects the inviting user can access. Cross-project queries that traverse outside that scope are forbidden, even when technically possible.
rule.rationale: Permission inheritance is per-project; treating MCP as a global key collapses the model.

rule.statement: An org MUST run exactly one Rovo MCP installation (one URL, one auth domain). Multiple parallel installations fragment audit trails and confuse permission inheritance.
rule.rationale: Two MCPs running in parallel split audit logs and let an attacker who breaches one bypass the other's review.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
